description: There is no context output for this command.
examples:
Command Example
!threat-grid-url-to-file urls=www.google.com
Human Readable Output
20. Get rate limits for an organization
Get rate limits applied to an organization. ThreatGrid employs a simple rate limiting method for sample submissions by specifying the number of samples which can be submitted within some variable time period by an entire organization and/or per a license basis. Multiple rate limits can be employed to form overlapping submission limits. For example, 20 submissions per hour AND 400 per day.
Base Command
threat-grid-organization-get-rate-limit
Input

!threat-grid-url-to-file urls=www.google.com
Human Readable Output
20. Get rate limits for an organization
Get rate limits applied to an organization. ThreatGrid employs a simple rate limiting method for sample submissions by specifying the number of samples which can be submitted within some variable time period by an entire organization and/or per a license basis. Multiple rate limits can be employed to form overlapping submission limits. For example, 20 submissions per hour AND 400 per day.
Base Command
threat-grid-organization-get-rate-limit
Input
Argument Name
name: threat-grid-organization-get-rate-limit

outputs.description: Array of array(s) representing submission(s) per minute(s) or the string"nil" to clear the value. Example: [[5, 1440]], which represents 5 samples per day. This field represent the minutes. ThreatGrid.User.RateLimit.Samples Array of array(s) representing submission(s) per minute(s) or the string"nil" to clear the value. Example: [[5, 1440]], which represents 5 samples per day. This field represent the number of samples allowed.
outputs.name: ThreatGrid.User.RateLimit.Minutes

outputs.description: The number of seconds to wait for a submission to be uploaded on the platform ThreatGrid.User.RateLimit.SubmissionsAvailable The number of submissions available for the entire organization
outputs.name: ThreatGrid.User.RateLimit.SubmissionWaitSeconds
